title: Xander Bogaerts talks Roman Anthony, pressure from fans in his return to Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Xander Bogaerts returned to Fenway Park as a Padres player, reflecting on changes since his Red Sox tenure.
Bogaerts expressed respect for Roman Anthony, a Red Sox outfielder contracted for $130 million over eight years.
Bogaerts acknowledged pressures of playing in Boston, noting the high expectations and winning culture among fans.

title: Step into Boston’s past this season
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston's Freedom Trail attracts over 4 million visitors annually, connecting 16 historical sites along a 2.5-mile path.
Six Flags New England to debut Quantum Accelerator, a dual-launch straddle coaster, available April 17 to all guests.
Restaurant Week Portsmouth & the Seacoast offers special menus April 9-18, with more than 30 restaurants participating.

title: Singer Vanessa Carlton’s noise complaint against R.I. neighbor ends up before state Supreme Court
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Vanessa Carlton and husband John McCauley contested their Warwick neighbors' noise levels at Rhode Island's Supreme Court.
A 2024 injunction ordered neighbors not to exceed 60 decibels after noise complaints from Carlton's family.
Neighbors appealed, disputing the injunction and noise claims; Rhode Island's Supreme Court has yet to rule.

title: Delta is adding winter service between Boston and Rome in 2027
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Delta Air Lines will add winter service between Boston and Rome beginning in January 2027.
The winter flights will operate four times a week, resuming daily service in March.
Fares for the early 2027 Boston-Rome flights go on sale April 4, 2026.

title: He’s running his second Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women’s Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Joe Greeley, 30, from Charlestown, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Stepping Strong team.
Greeley was inspired to run again after experiencing FOMO at the 2025 Boston Marathon.
He previously ran in 2024 for Brigham and Women's Hospital charity, Stepping Strong.

title: ‘Real Housewives of Rhode Island’: Everything that happened in Episode 1
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Bravo's 'Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premiered with a 90-minute episode featuring local cast members and drama-filled storylines.
The episode introduced Providence's Alicia Carmody, cannabis business owners Liz McGraw and Gerry, and throuple debates from Kelsey Swanson.
Rosie Woods DiMare and Jo-Ellen Tiberi clashed at a picnic, sparking conflicts over rumors and past grievances.

title: 8 gorgeous places in New England to see daffodils this spring
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Newport, R.I., hosts Daffodil Days with 1.5 million blooms, offering trolley tours, bike rides, and a dog parade in April.
Nantucket's flower-themed festival from April 24-26 includes a daffodil show, hat contest, and parades featuring kids and antique cars.
Meriden, Conn.'s Daffodil Festival on May 2-3 at Hubbard Park features 600,000 daffodils, live music, fireworks, and a craft fair.

title: Watch the Red Sox Opening Day ceremony honoring 1986 team
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox honored the 1986 American League pennant-winning team in their 2026 home opener ceremony at Fenway Park.
Twenty-five players from the 1986 team attended the ceremony, including Wade Boggs and Jim Rice.
Boston played against the San Diego Padres and featured music from Boston native Nik Walker without a planned military flyover.

title: Review & setlist: From flames to twerk-offs, Cardi B commands TD Garden
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Cardi B performed a sold-out show at TD Garden as part of her 'Little Miss Drama' tour.
The two-hour show included 37 songs, elaborate pyrotechnics, and multiple costume changes.
The concert featured a $2,000 'twerk-off' and highlighted Cardi B's interaction with the energetic crowd.

title: Emmy winner Amy Poehler spotted at popular Somerville coffee shop
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Amy Poehler was spotted at Diesel Cafe in Somerville, a popular spot for local and visiting celebrities.
The cafe shared a social media post featuring Poehler with an employee who recognized her.
Poehler is a Burlington native, Boston College alum, and known for her roles on 'Parks and Recreation' and 'SNL.'
